hooks.command: bash -c 'M=$(git branch --merged main 2>/dev/null | grep -v "[*]" | grep -vw main | xargs); T=$(git branch 2>/dev/null | grep -vw main | grep -cv "[*]"); if [ -n "$M" ]; then echo "🌿 BRANCH HYGIENE: merged into main but not deleted: $M -- run: git branch -d $M (see git-and-focus-discipline.md)."; elif [ "$T" -ge 10 ]; then echo "🌿 BRANCH HYGIENE: $T local branches. Prune merged ones (git branch --merged main) and finish or close stale ones before starting new work. Use bash scripts/branch-check.sh to spot collisions."; else echo OK; fi'

bash -c 'CHANGELOG="docs/changelogs/CHANGELOG.md"; if [ ! -f "$CHANGELOG" ]; then echo "OK"; exit 0; fi; LAST_MOD=$(git log -1 --format=%H -- "$CHANGELOG" 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$LAST_MOD" ]; then echo "OK"; exit 0; fi; COMMITS_SINCE=$(git rev-list --count "$LAST_MOD"..HEAD 2>/dev/null); if [ "$COMMITS_SINCE" -ge 10 ]; then echo "📝 CHANGELOG BEHIND: $COMMITS_SINCE commits since last changelog update. Consolidate recent changes into docs/changelogs/CHANGELOG.md grouped by feature (not per-commit). Run: git log --oneline $LAST_MOD..HEAD"; else echo "OK"; fi'

bash -c 'FIXES=$(git log --oneline -5 2>/dev/null | grep -c "^[a-f0-9]\+ fix"); if [ "$FIXES" -ge 3 ]; then echo "⚠️ FIX SPIRAL DETECTED: $FIXES consecutive fix commits found in last 5 commits. BEFORE attempting another fix: 1) Map ALL code paths through the affected system. 2) Document what fix #1 and #2 did and why they failed. 3) Identify the ROOT CAUSE, not another symptom. See change-discipline.md Fix Depth Rule."; else echo "OK"; fi'

bash -c 'B=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$B" ] && [ "$B" != "main" ] && [ -n "$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)" ]; then echo "🎯 FOCUS GUARD: uncommitted work in progress on branch $B. If this new request is UNRELATED to that work, do NOT start it - append it to docs/backlog/INBOX.md, tell the user it is noted, and finish the current task first (code + tests + commit + merge + delete). Divert only if the user explicitly says so. See git-and-focus-discipline.md."; else echo OK; fi'

bash -c 'B=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$B" ] || [ "$B" = "main" ]; then echo OK; exit 0; fi; HAS_UI=$(git diff --name-only main..HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -cE "\.(tsx|jsx|css|scss|html)$" || true); HAS_API=$(git diff --name-only main..HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -cE "(routes|api|handlers|endpoints)" || true); HAS_AUTH=$(git diff --name-only main..HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -ciE "(auth|login|session|token|oauth)" || true); COMMITS=$(git log --oneline main..HEAD 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d " "); if [ "$COMMITS" -ge 5 ]; then SUGGEST=""; if [ "$HAS_UI" -ge 2 ]; then SUGGEST="$SUGGEST /review-ux-live (UI changes detected)"; fi; if [ "$HAS_AUTH" -ge 1 ]; then SUGGEST="$SUGGEST /review-code-security (auth-related changes)"; elif [ "$HAS_API" -ge 1 ]; then SUGGEST="$SUGGEST /review-code-security (API changes)"; fi; if [ -n "$SUGGEST" ]; then echo "💡 REVIEW SUGGESTION: Your branch has ${COMMITS} commits. Consider running:${SUGGEST}. Type /review-guide for help choosing."; else echo OK; fi; else echo OK; fi'

bash -c 'if [ -f scripts/session-guard.sh ]; then OUT=$(bash scripts/session-guard.sh 2>/dev/null | grep "⚠️"); if [ -n "$OUT" ]; then echo "$OUT"; echo "See session-isolation.md - do not run destructive or cross-repo git."; else echo OK; fi; else echo OK; fi'

bash -c 'B=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); case "$B" in fix/*) A=$(git rev-list --count main..HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "${A:-0}" -lt 3 ]; then echo "🔎 VARIANT SEARCH: on $B. After you identify the root cause, search every other call site for the same pattern AND the same defect class elsewhere, fix ALL instances in this branch, and document: \"Variant search: checked [locations]. Found [N] additional instances.\" The reported instance is rarely the only one. See git-and-focus-discipline.md."; else echo OK; fi;; *) echo OK;; esac'
IN=$(cat); printf '%s' "$IN" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty' | grep -qE '(^|[^a-z])git commit|git-commit-push\.sh' && cat .claude/hooks/prompts/changelog-maintenance.txt || echo OK

bash -c 'if [ -d .claude ]; then NEW=$(find .kiro/steering .kiro/hooks .kiro/agents .kiro/prompts .kiro/skills -type f -newer .claude/CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null | head -1); if [ -n "$NEW" ]; then echo "🔁 CLAUDE EXPORT STALE: .kiro/ changed after .claude/ was generated. Run: bash scripts/export-to-claude.sh && git add .claude (see versioning.md release checklist)."; else echo OK; fi; else echo OK; fi'
bash -c 'mkdir -p logs && uvx ruff check --fix . 2>&1 | tee logs/ruff_check.log; exit ${PIPESTATUS[0]}'

bash -c 'if [ -f package.json ] && grep -q '"files"' package.json 2>/dev/null; then echo "=== npm pack dry-run ==="; npm pack --dry-run 2>&1 | tail -20; elif [ -f pyproject.toml ]; then echo "OK - verify pyproject.toml includes are correct"; else echo "OK"; fi'

bash -c 'B=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$B" ] && [ "$B" != "main" ] && [ -n "$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)" ]; then N=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d " "); echo "💾 CHECKPOINT: $N uncommitted change(s) on branch $B. Do NOT end here - commit a meaningful checkpoint (compiles + affected tests pass + lint clean) or a defensive checkpoint: commit, or stash. Work left in the working tree evaporates at the context boundary. See git-and-focus-discipline.md."; else echo OK; fi'

bash -c 'if [ -f tsconfig.json ]; then npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20; elif [ -f pyproject.toml ] && command -v ruff &>/dev/null; then ruff check . --select E,F --no-fix 2>&1 | head -20; else echo "OK"; fi'
